rules.kickers: Tiny mono kicker `(0X) — Label` in terracotta, uppercase, tracked wide.
rules.headlines: Giant serif headline with one italic terracotta word. Tight letter spacing.
rules.watermark: Huge faint rotated watermark word in background (e.g., text-[13rem] opacity-[0.04]).
rules.body_text: Generous line height, readable, left-aligned, muted_dark color.

layout_rules.avatar_space: Generous negative space (~38% column offset on desktop) to prevent obscuring the persistent floating 3D avatar.
layout_rules.asymmetry: Use asymmetric left/right offsets to create visual tension and an editorial feel.
layout_rules.spacing: Extremely generous padding between sections (py-32 or py-48).

motion_and_interactions.reveals: Framer Motion blur-in and subtle y-translate reveals when sections scroll into view.
motion_and_interactions.cursor: Custom cursor hover states using `data-cursor='hover'` on interactive elements.
motion_and_interactions.scroll: Parallax effects on images and background watermarks linked to scroll progress.

components_to_build.name: About
components_to_build.layout: Asymmetric split. Left offset for bio paragraph. Right offset for count-up stats.
components_to_build.motion: Text lines reveal from bottom staggered. Stats count up smoothly using Framer Motion `useInView` and `animate`.
features: CountUpStats (e.g., Years of Experience, Projects Delivered)

components_to_build.name: Skills
components_to_build.layout: Minimalist interactive accordion list of skill groups.
components_to_build.motion: Accordion expands smoothly, revealing pill-shaped tool tags inside. Smooth height transition.
features: SkillAccordion (Grouped by Frontend, Backend, Cloud, etc.)

components_to_build.name: Experience
components_to_build.layout: Vertical timeline with a left-aligned scroll-progress line. Cards are offset to the right.
components_to_build.motion: The vertical line fills with terracotta color mapped to `useScroll`. Cards fade and slide up as they enter viewport.

components_to_build.name: Travel
components_to_build.layout: Horizontal sticky scroll gallery. Parallax images representing places lived/visited (Mumbai, Regina, Aspirational).
components_to_build.motion: Sticky container that translates horizontally based on vertical scroll. Images inside cards have a slight counter-parallax effect to feel like 3D depth.

components_to_build.name: StyleFashion
components_to_build.layout: Magazine editorial masonry grid. Overlapping images, varying aspect ratios, vast negative space.
components_to_build.motion: Images reveal with staggered blur-in. Subtle parallax speeds differ per image. Hovering images scales them slightly and shows a custom cursor.
features: FloatingMonoCaption (small tracked-out mono captions next to images)

accessibility.contrast: Ensure text over panels meets APCA/WCAG standards. Use ink_text on background_base.
accessibility.motion: Respect prefers-reduced-motion for all scroll and reveal animations.
accessibility.testing: All interactive elements (accordion toggles, horizontal scroll containers, links) MUST include `data-testid` attributes.

image_urls.description: Mumbai cityscape - white and brown concrete building near body of water
Mumbai coastal view - people walking on side walk beside beach
Regina / Canada landscape - Breathtaking view of rugged mountains and meadows
Fashionable caucasian woman in white suit holding red lifebuoy
A striking portrait of a person in a bold outfit and hat

instructions_to_main_agent: DO NOT invent a new color palette. Use the EXISTING Light Neutral Editorial theme strictly (background_base: #EFEBE3, terracotta_accent: #BF5537, etc).
Maintain the layout structure keeping in mind the persistent 3D avatar; ensure sections have generous negative space (e.g., lg:ml-[38%]) where needed.
Use standard HTML + Tailwind for structural layouts. Shadcn UI can be used for forms/logic, but visual presentation must be completely custom and editorial.
Build a robust `HorizontalStickyScroll` component for the Travel section using Framer Motion `useScroll` and `useTransform`.

For the Style/Fashion section, avoid standard generic grids. Use overlapping absolute positioning within a relative container, or complex CSS Grid with empty cells, simulating an artful magazine spread.
Use the provided image URLs as high-quality placeholders for the Travel and Style sections.
